$comment: MCP tool contract for `unstick`. The output schema doubles as the model-hop schema (single pass, so per-pass and aggregate coincide). The sanitized (Anthropic-grammar) form is derived at registration; the non-empty and no-restatement rules on next_step are code-enforced after local validation.
name: unstick
description: Break a stuck loop by committing to one concrete next step. Call when you have a goal, you have tried things, and you are producing plausible motion that goes nowhere. Provide the goal, where you are blocked, and what you already tried; you get back exactly one immediately actionable step with a rationale - never a menu of options, never a plan. An external frame breaks the loop you cannot see from inside.

goal.description: What you are ultimately trying to accomplish. Arg-drop fallback: if your client cannot reliably serialize `blocked` (it arrives missing), append it here as `||BLOCKED|| <the blocker>` — the server recovers it and strips the block.

blocked.description: Where you are stuck right now - the immediate blocker, error, or dead end. Advertised optional (arg-drop tolerance): the server recovers it from a `||BLOCKED||` block in `goal` when omitted, and still rejects a genuinely-empty blocker.

tried.description: Approaches already attempted. The returned step will not restate any of these.

next_step.description: Exactly one concrete, immediately actionable step. Never multiple alternatives, never a plan.

rationale.description: Why this step breaks the loop, in one or two sentences.

watch_for.description: One pitfall likely to derail the step, or null.
